title: Apparatuses, methods and systems for tethering 3-d virtual elements to digital content
abstract: Aspects of the disclosed apparatuses, methods and systems provide tethering 3-D virtual elements in digital content, extracting tethered 3-D virtual elements, and manipulating the extracted 3-D virtual elements in a virtual 3-D space.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A computer implemented method of embedding virtual elements in digital content, the method comprising: parsing digital content of a document; detecting an tethered virtual element in the content of the document; accessing the tethered virtual element; and rendering a three-dimensional (3-D) representation of the tethered virtual element in a 3- D virtual workspace presenting the digital content. 2. The method of claim 1 wherein the rendered 3-D virtual element may be manipulated in the 3-D workspace using a display system. 3. The method of claim 1 wherein the accessing includes downloading a model of the virtual element from a server system. 4. The method of claim 1 wherein the rendered virtual element is a low-fidelity virtual element tethered to the content of the document. 5. The method of claim 4 wherein content is rendered in a virtual 3-D space and the low- fidelity virtual element is rendered in a bounding volume tethered to the rendered 3-D virtual content. 6. The method of claim 5 further comprising detecting the low-fidelity virtual element outside of the bounding volume and rendering a 3-D representation of a high-fidelity virtual element in place of virtual element in response. 7. The method of claim 1 wherein the document is an HTML document. 8. A computer system comprising: a storage device; an interface; and 

 one or more client processing devices configured by machine-readable instructions to: parse the digital content of a document stored in the storage device; detect an tethered virtual element in the content of the document; access the tethered virtual element via the interface; and rendering a three-dimensional (3-D) representation of the tethered virtual element in a 3 workspace presenting the digital content.
